title: vignea maritima subsp. setina
intro: Vignea maritima subsp. setina, also known as sea lavender, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of sea lavender include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. In herbal preparations, sea lavender is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be applied topically to the skin or inhaled to reap its benefits.
